name: Consumer Credit Counseling Services Of Northern Illinois
description_short: NFCC-certified non-profit offering free and low-cost financial counseling, housing assistance, debt management, and bankruptcy education to Northern Illinois residents.
description_long:
Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Northern Illinois (CCCSNI) is a non-profit credit counseling organization serving the Northern Illinois region. The organization is staffed by NFCC-certified financial educators and housing counselors, including Executive Director Donna Rasmussen (with the organization since 2019) and counselors with credentials from HUD and the National Foundation for Credit Counseling. The team includes professionals with backgrounds in banking, finance, military service, and community outreach.

CCCSNI offers a comprehensive suite of financial counseling services designed to address multiple consumer pain points. These include Financial Counseling for general debt management, Housing Counseling (including reverse mortgage guidance for seniors 62+), Debt Management Programs that serve as liaisons with creditors to establish repayment plans, Bankruptcy Counseling to explore alternatives and navigate the filing process, Student Loan Counseling for borrowers struggling with repayment, and Credit Report Analysis to identify and correct errors and improve credit scores. The organization also provides financial literacy education and community outreach.

CCCSNI distinguishes itself through several specific achievements and characteristics. The organization was selected as Federal Home Loan Bank Chicago's first Success Story in their Housing Counseling Resource Program (HCRP) as of August 2024, indicating institutional recognition of their work. They were invited to the Racial and Economic Housing Equity Solutions Summit, suggesting a focus on equitable financial access. Staff expertise spans both technical financial knowledge (team members hold degrees in Finance, Psychology, and Management from institutions like DePaul University and Eastern Illinois University) and practical banking experience, with at least one counselor working full-time in banking while counseling.

The honest assessment is that while CCCSNI offers legitimate, certified counseling services with strong credentials and institutional recognition, the website provides limited transparent information about pricing structures, wait times, service availability across Northern Illinois communities, or measurable outcome data. As a free-help organization, they are appropriate for consumers seeking legitimate, non-predatory guidance, but prospective clients should verify service availability in their specific area and clarify any costs before engaging.
pros: NFCC-certified counselors with HUD certifications ensure professional, standardized guidance
Completely non-profit model with no financial incentive to recommend debt settlement or predatory products
Specialized expertise in reverse mortgages for seniors 62+, addressing specific demographic needs
Recognized by Federal Home Loan Bank Chicago as a Success Story in Housing Counseling
Offers debt management programs that negotiate directly with creditors on behalf of clients
Comprehensive breadth of services (6+ distinct counseling specializations) under one organization
Team includes full-time banking professional providing practical real-world financial knowledge
cons: Website lacks transparent pricing information—unclear whether all services are truly free or have fees
No published data on average outcomes, success rates, or client satisfaction metrics
Geographic service area ('Northern Illinois') is vague; specific county/zip code coverage not stated
Limited information about typical wait times, appointment availability, or service modalities (phone/in-person/online)
No testimonials or case studies provided on website to demonstrate actual client results
best_for: Homeowners struggling with mortgage payments, refinancing decisions, or reverse mortgage options
Consumers with multiple debts seeking a creditor liaison to negotiate lower payments without settlement companies
Recent graduates or mid-career borrowers with overwhelming federal or private student loan balances
Individuals considering bankruptcy who want to explore alternatives before filing
diagnosis: CCCSNI is best for Northern Illinois residents seeking legitimate, non-profit credit counseling from certified professionals without sales pressure or predatory product recommendations. The main caveat is that the website does not clearly specify geographic service boundaries, pricing transparency, or verifiable outcome data—prospective clients should contact directly to confirm service availability in their area and clarify any associated costs before committing.
services: Financial Counseling (general debt management and budgeting)
Housing Counseling (mortgage, homeownership, rental, and reverse mortgage guidance)
Debt Management Program (creditor negotiation and repayment plan development)
Bankruptcy Counseling (education and exploration of alternatives)
Student Loan Counseling (repayment strategy and option exploration)
Credit Report Analysis (error identification and credit score improvement)
Financial Literacy Education (community outreach and workshops)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: NFCC-certified non-profit offering free financial counseling, housing assistance, debt management, and bankruptcy education.
